public_reputation: Contract-clerk burglar who steals licenses, ward keys, and registry tokens more often than coin.
manner: Reads as a functional local operator shaped by place, duty, and the practical customs around them. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a spellthief still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of spellthief in and around Guildheart Hub, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Guildheart Hub workable through the obligations of spellthief while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in The Union.
hidden_pressure: Must keep their piece of the world working while larger systems push stress downward. Around Guildheart Hub, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of spellthief visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Marren Toll can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.
Marren Toll needs help with a problem in Guildheart Hub that larger systems have pushed down onto ordinary hands.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained.
public_signal: Marren Toll carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Guildheart Hub: the part of Guildheart Hub where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break
